function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux pane contents (live + archived)"
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
  set -l archive_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/archive

  # Default: fzf over pane content files, optional query pre-fills search
  if not test -d "$pane_dir"
    echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
    return
  end

  set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
  set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" "$archive_dir" 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
          --height=40% \
          --query="$query" \
          --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
          --preview-window=right:60%)

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # work--0--0.txt or work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt → sess=work, widx=0
  set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" \
    | string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' \
    | string replace '.txt' '')
  set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    bat --style=plain "$selected" 2>/dev/null; or less "$selected"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

content.thinking:
The error is on line 25:
```
| string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' \
```

The problem is that `string replace` with the `-r` (regex) flag should work, but the issue might be that the pattern `--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$` starts with `--` which Fish interprets as "end of options" marker OR as an unknown flag because `--` after `-r` is not just a double dash separator.

Wait, actually in Fish's `string replace`, the syntax is:
```
string replace [--all] [--filter] [--ignore-case] [--quiet] [--regex] [--] PATTERN REPLACEMENT [STRING ...]


```

So when you have `string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$'`, Fish parses it as:
- `-r` = regex flag (OK)
- `--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$` - this starts with `--`, so Fish thinks it's another option like `--regex` or similar

The issue is that the pattern starts with `--`, which Fish's argument parser interprets as a flag rather than the actual regex pattern. I need to add an explicit `--` separator after the `-r` flag to tell Fish that everything after it is an argument, not an option.
```

content.text:
The fix: Fish's `string replace` parser sees the pattern `--\d{8}...` starting with `--` and tries to interpret it as a long option flag, failing with "unknown option". Adding `--` after `-r` tells the parser that no more flags follow, so the next argument is treated as the literal pattern.

You'll need to rebuild/switch home-manager to pick up the change, or temporarily source the fixed version directly.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
function _tsh_function --description "Search tmux pane contents (live + archived)"
  set -l pane_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
  set -l archive_dir ~/.local/share/tmux/archive

  # Default: fzf over pane content files, optional query pre-fills search
  if not test -d "$pane_dir"
    echo "No pane content store found at $pane_dir"
    return
  end

  set -l query (string join ' ' $argv)
  set -l selected (rg -l -- "$query" "$pane_dir" "$archive_dir" 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="pane-search> " \
          --height=40% \
          --query="$query" \
          --preview="rg -n -- '$query' {} 2>/dev/null | head -80" \
          --preview-window=right:60%)

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  # work--0--0.txt or work--0--0--20260226-103000.txt → sess=work, widx=0
  set -l fname (string replace -r '.*/' '' "$selected" \
    | string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' \
    | string replace '.txt' '')
  set -l parts (string split -- '--' $fname)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if not tmux has-session -t "$sess" 2>/dev/null
    bat --style=plain "$selected" 2>/dev/null; or less "$selected"
    return
  end

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx" 2>/dev/null
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end

input.command:
gh pr create \
  --title "fix(fish): add -- separator in _tsh_function string replace pattern" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Changes
- Add `--` end-of-options separator to `string replace -r` call in `_tsh_function`

## Problem
Fish's `string replace` parser treats `--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$` as an unknown flag because the pattern starts with `--`, producing:
```
string replace: --\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$: unknown option
```

## Fix
Add `--` after `-r` to signal end of options, so the pattern is parsed as a literal argument:
```diff
-    | string replace -r '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' \
+    | string replace -r -- '--\d{8}-\d{6}\.txt$' '' \
```

Generated with Claude Code
EOF
)" \
  --base main
